narrative:
Johan Vázquez is a Centre-Back representing Mexico at the tournament. His overall rating stands at 7.1/10, placing him within the top 7% of centre-backs participating. This rating, which spans a range of 6.71–7.48, is largely influenced by his club form. A substantial 72% of his rating derives from his performances at club level over the last two seasons.

Vázquez has played all 360 available minutes at this World Cup, showcasing his importance. He excels in defensive duties, averaging 4.75 clearances per 90 minutes. His offensive contributions are notably low, with just 0.025 expected goals and 0.25 shots per 90 minutes. He also contributes 0.75 tackles and 0.5 interceptions per 90 minutes for his team.

Vázquez serves as a foundational defensive presence for his national team, anchoring their backline. Scouts and fans should continue to monitor his consistent defensive output, especially his ability to relieve pressure with clearances. His strong club form, which heavily influences his current rating, suggests he is a reliable and experienced performer for Mexico.
